steps.text: Define the variable relationship: $x$ is the price of a ticket sold during the event. The price before the event is $\$3$ more than during, so the before-event price is $x + 3$.
Express total revenue: the team sells at least 140 tickets before the event at $(x+3)$ each, and at least 220 tickets during the event at $x$ each. Total revenue $= 140(x+3) + 220x$.
Apply the minimum requirement: the team needs to raise at least $\$5{,}820$, so the inequality is $140(x+3) + 220x \ge 5{,}820$.

Evaluate all choices:
  • A: $140(x+3) + 220x \le 5{,}820$ — uses the correct revenue expression but the wrong direction; "at least" requires $\ge$, not $\le$. Eliminated.
  • B: $140(x+3) + 220x \ge 5{,}820$ — correct revenue expression and correct direction. That matches choice B — B is correct.
  • C: $140(x-3) + 220x \le 5{,}820$ — subtracts 3 from $x$ for the before-event ticket price, but the before-event price should be $x+3$ (higher, not lower). Also uses wrong direction. Eliminated.
  • D: $140(x-3) + 220x \ge 5{,}820$ — correct direction but wrong price relationship; subtracting 3 from before-event price is backwards. Eliminated.
